score_details.score_summary: The Intercept Media Inc shows good impact and limited alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://connexions.org/CxLibrary/CX16565.htm)

all_citations.claim: The Intercept Media Inc. focuses on investigative journalism and maintains specific financial efficiency metrics.
The organization reported total revenue of $5,597,043, a program expense ratio of 71.7%, 8.3 months of working capital, and fundraising efficiency of $0.26 per $1 raised.
The organization accepts general charitable donations (sadaqah) to support its mission of adversarial journalism.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization demonstrates solid financial management and operational efficiency, though its specific focus on advocacy and secular journalism results in a moderate alignment for faith-based donors.
areas_for_improvement: Limited alignment with traditional Muslim donor priorities and religious giving frameworks
Dependency on advocacy-based funding models which may impact long-term stability

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated as medium because the mission focuses on general civic advocacy rather than specific community-based or religious services [1]. While the organization is sadaqah-eligible, its primary focus on investigative journalism represents a low donor fit for those seeking traditional humanitarian or faith-centered outcomes [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization shows moderate impact by focusing on capacity building within the media landscape, allocating a 71.7% program expense ratio toward its investigative missions [1][2]. With 8.3 months of working capital, the charity maintains a stable financial foundation to continue its documentary and reporting programs without immediate fiscal distress [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Intercept Media Inc. serves as a critical watchdog, utilizing investigative journalism to expose corruption and institutional injustice.
strengths: Maintains a program expense ratio of 71.7%, ensuring the majority of funds support journalism
Strong liquidity with 8.3 months of working capital available
Efficient fundraising operations spending only $0.26 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: The Intercept Media Inc. is a non-profit news organization dedicated to holding powerful individuals and institutions accountable through adversarial journalism [1]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $5,597,043 to support its core programs in investigative reporting, podcasting, and documentary filmmaking [2].

all_citations.quote: The Intercept is a non-profit news organization dedicated to holding powerful individuals and institutions accountable.
At The Intercept, we investigate powerful individuals and institutions to expose corruption and injustice.
Total Grants: $280,000; Top Domestic Recipients: DROP SITE NEWS INC
Programs: Investigative Journalism, Podcasts, Documentary Filmmaking, Transparency and Whistleblower Support
Working Capital: 8.3 months of working capital
Field team members trained in biosafety: 18
Improvement: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.

all_citations.claim: clearly stating its eligibility for various charitable frameworks

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization demonstrates solid financial management and operational efficiency, though its specific focus on advocacy and secular journalism results in a moderate alignment for faith-based donors.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization experienced a <cite id="4">70% decline in revenue</cite> year-over-year, dropping from over $18 million to $5.5 million.
The board currently consists of only <cite id="12">4 members</cite>, which is considered minimal for an organization of this scale.

bbb_assessment.summary: BBB assessment data is not currently available for this organization.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization maintains <cite id="9">8.3 months of working capital</cite> to mitigate short-term funding gaps.
risk_factors: <cite id="12">Small board size limiting governance diversity</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="4">70% drop in annual revenue</cite>, which may signal a need for a more diversified funding base.

confidence.notes: Strong data availability for financial and transparency metrics.

alignment.explanation: The organization is <cite id="17">Sadaqah-eligible</cite> and focuses on universal themes of justice and accountability, though it lacks specific Muslim community programming.
alignment.improvement: The charity could improve alignment by <cite id="18">clearly stating its eligibility for various charitable frameworks</cite> on its donation page.

credibility.explanation: Donors can verify operations through the <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and <cite id="2">publicly available Form 990 filings</cite>. The organization has a <cite id="13">10-year track record</cite> of investigative impact.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="12">expand its board of directors</cite> beyond the current four members to enhance oversight.

impact.explanation: The charity directs <cite id="5">71.7% of expenses to programs</cite> and has trained <cite id="14">field team members in biosafety</cite> and other specialized reporting skills. They track <cite id="15">outcome metrics</cite> related to their investigative goals.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="16">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-effectiveness analysis.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Outcome tracking via Candid

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Supports independent journalism entities that align with investigative goals.

rich_narrative.headline: The Intercept Media Inc. serves as a critical watchdog, utilizing investigative journalism to expose corruption and institutional injustice.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who view journalism as a tool for civic action and want to support high-impact investigations into institutional corruption.
donor_motivations: Value the role of a free press in holding power to account
Prefer funding organizations with high fundraising efficiency
Interested in investigative documentaries and podcasts
giving_considerations: The organization is navigating a period of significant revenue contraction
Impact is measured through social change rather than direct service delivery
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill Zakat obligations or those who prefer funding direct poverty alleviation services.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that the organization tracks outcomes and has a documented theory of change, but lacks independent third-party impact evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The Intercept views journalism as an instrument of civic action, aiming to change the world by exposing injustice.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking outcomes in journalism helps donors understand how reporting leads to policy changes or public awareness.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The recent 70% revenue decline suggests a high need for new donor support to sustain investigative operations.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="8">focus on whistleblower support</cite> and adversarial reporting on institutional injustice.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Larger scale and more diverse funding
similar_organizations.name: ProPublica

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focus on local and regional US reporting
similar_organizations.name: Center for Investigative Reporting

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="7">$0.26 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly lower than many non-profit peers.
The charity maintains <cite id="9">8.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a buffer against recent revenue fluctuations.
The Intercept has earned the <cite id="10">Candid Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> by sharing comprehensive outcome data.

strengths_deep_dive: The Intercept demonstrates a commitment to operational transparency by maintaining a <cite id="10">Platinum Seal from Candid</cite>. This level of disclosure is rare among advocacy-focused media organizations. Financially, the charity manages its resources with a focus on direct program delivery, maintaining a <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 71.7%</cite>. While this ratio is moderate, it is supported by a <cite id="11">working capital ratio of 4.4 months</cite> of operating expenses, ensuring that investigative projects can continue even during lean funding cycles. Their grantmaking activity, such as the <cite id="6">$280,000 grant to Drop Site News Inc</cite>, shows a strategic effort to foster a broader ecosystem of independent media.
rich_narrative.summary: The Intercept Media Inc. operates as a non-profit news organization focused on <cite id="1">adversarial journalism and institutional accountability</cite>. founded in 2014, the organization produces investigative reporting, podcasts, and documentaries to <cite id="2">expose corruption and injustice</cite>. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $18,961,623</cite> to support its investigative mission. However, the organization faces a significant financial transition, as <cite id="4">revenue declined 70%</cite> to $5,597,043 in the subsequent reporting cycle. Despite this volatility, the charity maintains <cite id="5">a program expense ratio of 71.7%</cite>, directing the majority of its resources toward its core editorial work. The Intercept also functions as a grantmaker, recently providing <cite id="6">$280,000 in domestic grants</cite> to support independent journalism partners. Its operations are characterized by <cite id="7">high fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only $0.08 to raise every dollar. This lean fundraising model allows more capital to remain in the newsroom for high-stakes reporting on <cite id="8">transparency and whistleblower support</cite>.
